LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOAN HARTLEY MOTION FOR ORDER ENFORCING THE COURT'S JUDGMENT AND/OR RENEWED VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 10, 2009 PER RE-NOTICE. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT VALVERDE, GEORGE DIRECTOR TO THE DEPARMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES FOR THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOAN HARTLEY (RE-NOTICE) OF HEARING ON MOTION FOR ORDER ENFORCING THE COURT'S JUDGMENT AND/OR RENEWED VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: WRIT PETITION GRANTED AND THE MATTER IS REMANDED FOR THE AGENCY TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE EVIDENCE RELATING TO THE 2000 ALLEGED PRIOR CONVICTION IS SUFFICIENT TO QUALIFY AS A CALIFORNIA DRIVING UNDER THE INFLUENCE CONVICTION USING THE STANDARDS SET FORTH IN DRAEGER VS. REED (1999) 69 CAL. APP. 4TH 1511, 1521: 1)WISCONSIN LAW IS SUBSTANTIALLY THE SAME AS CALIFORNIA LAW ON THE SAME OFFENSE; 2)THE DESCRIPTION OF THE VIOLATION IS SUFFICIENT; AND, 3)THE INTERPRETATION AND ENFORCEMENT OF THE OTHER STATE'S LAW ARE SUBSTANTIALLY THE SAME AS CALIFORNIA LAW. EXHIBIT 11 SUPPORTING THE 2000 PRIOR CONVICTION CONTAINS WHAT APPEARS TO BE A POLICE REPORT THAT IS NOT AN ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT, AND DOES NOT ORDINARILY FORM PART OF THE RECORD OF CONVICTION. (SEE DROEGER AT PG. 1523). NO CLERK'S CERTIFICATION APPEARS TO INCORPORATE THIS DOCUMENT THAT FORMS THE BASIS FOR PETITIONER'S "DRIVING," AND THE REMAINING DOCUMENTS ARE AMBIGUOUS REGARDING "DRIVING" OR "OPERATING." SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE IS PRESENTED REGARDING THE 2006 PRIOR CONVICTION. EXHIBIT 12 CONTAINS THE JUDGMENT OF CONVICTION, AND THE CRIMINAL COMPLAINT THAT INCORPORATES THE STATEMENT OF PROBABLE CAUSE. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES DENIED, DMV'S ACTION NOT ARBITRARY OR CAPRICIOUS. (COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
NOTICE AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF RECEIPT OF VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE; MEMO OF P&A FILED BY PETITIONER HARTLEY, JOAN B

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JOAN HARTLEY'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE UNDER CCP SECTION 1094.5, VEHICLE CODE SECTION 13559 AND 14401 AND OTHER APPLICABLE LAW. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANT AND REMAND FOR FURTHER PROCEEDINGS CONSISTENT WITH THIS RULING. THE ADMINISTRATIVE DECISION OF OFFICER MEDINA REFLECTS THAT SHE DID NOT PROPERLY CONSIDER PETITIONER'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE OF THE WISCONSIN OPERATING WHILE INTOXICATED RECORDS, IN LIGHT OF, DRAEGER VS. REED, 69, CAL.APP. 4TH, 1511. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN, CSR #10276
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-23-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
(Re-Notice) Of Hearing On Motion For Order Enforcing The Court'S Judgement And/Or Renewed Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER JOAN HARTLEY (Re-Notice) Of Hearing On Motion For Order Enforcing The Court's Judgment and/or Renewed Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandate. Appearances: David A. Carrillo, Deputy Attorney General, (510) 622-2215 appearing for Calif. Dept. of Motor Vehicles. (The tentative ruling is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Writ petition granted and the matter is remanded for the agency to determine whether the evidence relating to the 2000 alleged prior conviction is sufficient to qualify as a California driving under the influence conviction using the standards set forth in Draeger vs. Reed (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 1511, 1521: 1)Wisconsin law is substantially the same as California law on the same offense; 2)the description of the violation is sufficient; and, 3)the interpretation and enforcement of the other state's law are substantially the same as California law. Exhibit 11 supporting the 2000 prior conviction contains what appears to be a police report that is not an abstract of judgment, and does not ordinarily form part of the record of conviction. (See Droeger at pg. 1523). No clerk's certification appears to incorporate this document that forms the basis for petitioner's "driving," and the remaining documents are ambiguous regarding "driving" or "operating." Sufficient admissible evidence is presented regarding the 2006 prior conviction. Exhibit 12 contains the judgment of conviction, and the criminal complaint that incorporates the statement of probable cause. Request for attorney fees denied, DMV's action not arbitrary or capricious. (Counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc Of Hearing On Verified Petitionfor Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER JOAN HARTLEY'S Notice of Hearing on Verified Petition for Writ of Mandate under CCP section 1094.5, Vehicle Code section 13559 and 14401 and other applicable law. . Appearances: Gordon M. Fauth, Jr., of the Litigation Law Group, (510) 238-9610, appearing for Petitioner, Joan Hartley, and moving party; David A. Carrillo, Deputy Attorney General, (510) 622-2215, appearing for Respondent, Department of Motor Vehicles for the State of California. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Grant and Remand for further proceedings consistent with this ruling. The Administrative Decision of Officer Medina reflects that she did not properly consider Petitioner's objections to evidence of the Wisconsin Operating While Intoxicated Records, in light of, Draeger vs. Reed, 69, Cal.App. 4th, 1511. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR 10276
